method: Landauer bound on information erased per preflight call

physics.formula: E_min = k_B · T · ln(2) per bit erased
physics.derivation: Landauer 1961: the irreversible erasure of one bit requires at least kT·ln(2) joules of energy dissipated as heat

bits_per_entry.note: Content proxy represents the information content of a typical 256-byte memory entry. Real content would be variable but 2KB is a reasonable central tendency.

frontier_points.note: frontier: highest safety-per-joule — governance is essentially free in thermodynamic terms

pareto_frontier_analysis.interpretation: Every BLOCK decision costs ~1.3e-16 joules in the Landauer minimum bound. Even a single prevented fintech failure ($670) represents a safety-per-joule ratio of 5e18 — governance is effectively free compared to the physical heat dissipation of the underlying compute.

gpt4_inference.description: rough estimate for one GPT-4 inference

aggregate_comparison.ratio: Sgraal is ~6.5e5 times cheaper thermodynamically than a single LLM call it governs
aggregate_comparison.implication: The Landauer overhead of memory governance is a 7-order-of-magnitude improvement over the decision it protects.

fleet_scale.interpretation: A 1000-agent fleet running Sgraal for a year erases less energy than a LED flash on a smartphone. Thermodynamic cost is not a budget constraint.

in_response.note: Included on every preflight response. Logged to audit_log.extra on BLOCK calls as bits_erased + landauer_joules fields.
